cards.definition: An informal, non-audited sourcing relationship where the buyer deals directly with the farmer or farmer group, cutting out middlemen, paying a negotiated price rather than a certification-mandated floor.
cards.analogy: This is essentially your model — and Black Baza's.
cards.why_it_matters: It's the foundation your brand's named-farm, named-price claims are built on.
cards.cloze: ___ is an informal sourcing relationship where the buyer deals directly with the farmer, cutting out middlemen.

cards.definition: An official third-party certification that guarantees a minimum floor price plus a social premium paid to the farmer group.
cards.analogy: A guaranteed minimum wage, certified by an outside auditor.
cards.why_it_matters: Worth knowing as a baseline — many specialty roasters already pay well above this floor without needing the certification.
cards.cloze: ___ is an official certification guaranteeing a minimum floor price plus a social premium to the farmer group.

cards.definition: A third-party certification that audits environmental and social farming standards, such as land use and worker conditions.
cards.analogy: An outside inspector checking a farm's environmental and labor practices against a checklist.
cards.why_it_matters: One of the certifications that matters more in export contexts than in direct, named-farm relationships.
cards.cloze: ___ certification audits environmental and social farming standards on the farm.

cards.definition: India's official organic certification, confirming the absence of synthetic inputs like chemical fertilizers or pesticides.
cards.analogy: The paperwork proving a farm didn't use synthetic chemicals — not a taste guarantee.
cards.why_it_matters: Many farmers grow organically without paying for this certification — worth checking with a farmer rather than assuming either way.
cards.cloze: ___ is India's official certification confirming a farm didn't use synthetic fertilizers or pesticides.
